examples: Their faces, as described in Twilight, "were all 32 devastatingly, inhumanly beautiful."
Also, forbidding, doctrinaire, witty, obsessed and almost inhumanly brave as her illness ground her along on her long passage to death.
The things they lift are so inhumanly heavy that they have no choice but to turn their bodies into massive counterweights.
In 2003 Schneider decided to invest in a fleet-wide "tactical planning simulator" that would use software algorithms to mimic the decision making of human dispatchers on an inhumanly large scale.
